extension 0..0Extension

value[x] 0..1CodeableConceptValue of extension

Binding: EMS Certification Level (ValueSet) (extensible)

Documentation for this format

name: EMSAgencyConfiguration
title: EMS Agency Configuration
description: NEMSIS dConfiguration: state, permitted procedures/medications with certification levels, protocols, specialty service capability, billing status, EMD provision/vendor, patient monitoring capability, crew call signs, and dispatch centers. Repeat the extension per configured state (dConfiguration.01).

element.definition: NEMSIS dConfiguration: state, permitted procedures/medications with certification levels, protocols, specialty service capability, billing status, EMD provision/vendor, patient monitoring capability, crew call signs, and dispatch centers. Repeat the extension per configured state (dConfiguration.01).

constraint.human: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

element.definition: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

slicing.description: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

element.definition: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.
element.comment: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions.  The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

element.definition: NEMSIS dConfiguration: state, permitted procedures/medications with certification levels, protocols, specialty service capability, billing status, EMD provision/vendor, patient monitoring capability, crew call signs, and dispatch centers. Repeat the extension per configured state (dConfiguration.01).

element.short: dConfiguration.01 - State Associated with this Configuration
element.comment: NEMSIS dConfiguration.01. Usage: Mandatory. 2-letter USPS abbreviation per NDR-004.

element.short: dConfiguration.06/.07 - EMS Agency Procedures with permitted certification levels
element.comment: NEMSIS dConfiguration.07 (SNOMED procedure code) with dConfiguration.06 (permitted certification levels). Repeat per procedure.

element.short: dConfiguration.06 - Certification level permitted to perform
element.comment: NEMSIS state certification/licensure levels; uses the eCrew.02 certification level codes.

element.short: dConfiguration.08/.09 - EMS Agency Medications with permitted certification levels
element.comment: NEMSIS dConfiguration.09 (RxNorm medication code) with dConfiguration.08 (permitted certification levels). Repeat per medication.

element.short: dConfiguration.08 - Certification level permitted to administer

element.comment: NEMSIS dConfiguration.10. Same 9914xxx protocol codes as eProtocols.01.

element.short: dConfiguration.11 - EMS Agency Specialty Service Capability
element.comment: NEMSIS dConfiguration.11. Usage: Recommended. Repeating (0..M).
NEMSIS dConfiguration.12. Usage: Optional. Whether the agency bills for service; NEMSIS YesNoValues mapped to boolean.

element.short: dConfiguration.13 - EMD Provided to EMS Agency Service Area
element.comment: NEMSIS dConfiguration.13. Usage: Mandatory.
NEMSIS dConfiguration.14. Usage: Recommended. Free-text vendor name. Repeating (0..M).
NEMSIS dConfiguration.15. Usage: Recommended. Repeating (0..M).
NEMSIS dConfiguration.16. Usage: Mandatory. Repeating (1..M).

element.short: dConfiguration.17 - Dispatch Center (CAD) Name or ID
element.comment: NEMSIS dConfiguration.17. Usage: Optional. Repeating (0..M).
